method: EO triangulated from league.json (15-man mini-league template = Haaland/B.Fernandes/Gabriel; differentials clustered at 6.7% = 1 of 15 managers) plus GW38 web signals. CRITICAL CORRECTION vs prior draft: Haaland is NOT out league-wide. Web confirms Haaland plays MCI v AVL (H) and is the GW38 top captain (~35%) with ~64% overall ownership. He is UNAVAILABLE ONLY TO THIS USER (held in squad with status UNAVAILABLE, cannot start). So for the field Haaland's EO is ~99%; for the user his contribution is forced to 0. This is the single biggest one-way rank risk and there is NO shield available.

effective_ownership.note: Field EO ~99% (64% own + 35% (C)). PLAYS for Man City at home v leaky Villa - 15 box shots + 7 big chances in last 4. User holds him but status UNAVAILABLE so he scores 0 for the user. Unfixable one-way downside.
2nd template + 2nd-most captained. Away at Brighton. User does not own - secondary blowup risk.
Template defender, owned but DOUBTFUL. If he sits, user blanks a covered template slot. Confirm pre-deadline; Munoz ready to swap.
Owned, vice-captain. Template-safe, high floor live armband alternative.
User's (C). Home v Leeds, on pens, double-digit in 2 of last 3 home games. Differential captain - only 10% of field captain him.
Sub-8% differential the user owns - small edge vs league.

template_risk.primary: Haaland (C) ~35% of field + 64% own, EO ~99%, PLAYS at home v leaky Villa. If he hauls, captain-Haaland managers gain ~16-30 pts the user CANNOT match because Haaland is unavailable to the user. Pure one-way downside.
template_risk.secondary: B.Fernandes ~26% captaincy away at Brighton. A Bruno haul widens the gap vs the 26% who captained him.
template_risk.blowup_scenario: Haaland double-digit + Bruno return = template managers +25-40 pts on the user in one GW. Probability moderate-high given fixtures.
template_risk.shield_assessment: NO shield exists - the user cannot field Haaland. The only counter is variance: out-haul via a differential captain (Bowen). Bowen (C) is the rank-chasing lever, NOT a fade target.

captain_shield.why: Normally the must-not-miss captain shield. FLAG FOR AWARENESS ONLY: the user physically cannot captain or field him (status UNAVAILABLE). There is no defensive play - sim-lab/gaffer must accept this gap is unfixable and instead maximise the user's own differential ceiling.

User's captain and the de facto shield-replacement. HIGH FLOOR: nailed, on pens, survival-desperate WHU at home v a Leeds side with nothing to play for. Only 10% captain him so a Bowen haul GAINS rank on the field. DO NOT fade - he is the user's primary differential-captain weapon. Keep (C) on Bowen unless a confirmed-injury override appears.

differentials.why: Pens + home + motivated WHU v Leeds. Best ceiling-per-ownership captain available to a manager who cannot field Haaland.
CRY forward, low EO, penalty/aerial threat. Differential the user owns vs template-heavy rivals.
NFO target-man, low EO, goal threat. Owned differential adding swing potential.
Sub-8% league differential the user owns. AVL away at Man City is tough so ceiling capped, but any return out-scores the field.

rank_risks.why: EO ~99%, 35% captaincy, PLAYS - but UNAVAILABLE to user. A Haaland haul loses the user ground to nearly the entire field and almost certainly the mini-league leader. Unfixable this GW - no transfer restores exposure.
DOUBTFUL template defender. If benched/injured the user blanks a slot the field returns on. Confirm lineup; have Munoz ready to swap in.
26%-captained template MID the user does not own. A Bruno haul compounds the Haaland gap.

league_position_analysis.verdict: NOT catchable. A 185-pt gap cannot close in a single final GW - max realistic one-GW swing between two managers is ~40-60 pts and only if the leader blanks AND the user triple-hauls. The leader almost certainly captains the available Haaland (template) which the user cannot field, so the realistic GW38 expectation is the gap WIDENING. Season is mathematically decided.
league_position_analysis.strategic_implication: Stop playing for the leader. GW38 = defend/improve 7th vs nearby managers and maximise raw points / overall rank for pride. Because the user cannot field Haaland, a safe template-mirror is impossible - the user is forced into a differential captain (Bowen) and should lean INTO variance, not away from it.

tc_chip_note: TC still available. Triple-Captaining Bowen is the only aggressive lever left. It will NOT close a 185-pt league gap, but for overall rank / max-points it is defensible: Bowen has a genuine haul fixture (home v Leeds, on pens) and only 10% of the field captain him, so a TC Bowen haul is high-variance upside with little to lose in a decided season. Risk: a Bowen blank wastes the chip. Decision belongs to gaffer/sim-lab; intel flags it as the single highest-ceiling play available given Haaland is off-limits.

differential_edge.net: User trades template safety (Haaland, Bruno) for low-owned forwards + a differential captain. HIGH-VARIANCE hand: it only wins the GW if Haaland/Bruno underperform AND Bowen/Mateta/Wood return. Season being decided, leaning into that variance is the correct posture.

recommendation: Season is decided - the 185-pt gap to Krisss. is NOT catchable in GW38, and Haaland (EO ~99%, 35% captaincy) PLAYS but is UNAVAILABLE to the user, so the user faces one-way template downside with NO shield. Therefore pivot to variance: keep the captain on Bowen (high floor, home v Leeds, on pens, only 10% captaincy = a haul gains rank) and seriously consider TRIPLE CAPTAIN Bowen as the last high-ceiling lever, since the chip is otherwise wasted on a decided season. Confirm Gabriel's fitness pre-deadline and bench him for Munoz if not starting. Do NOT chase the template - the user cannot field Haaland and mirroring a hand without him only locks in the loss.
